Doc: Yes — a ceramide-rich body lotion such as DERMACY’s Ceramide-3 Deep Nourishing Body Lotion can help repair and reinforce the skin’s natural barrier, especially if the skin is dry, sensitive, or compromised.
Label: Fact

Doc: Ceramides are lipid molecules naturally present in the outermost layer of the skin.
Label: Fact

Doc: They help maintain the “cement” between skin cells — sealing moisture in and blocking harmful irritants or allergens from entering.
Label: Fact

Doc: When skin loses ceramides (due to weather, harsh cleansers, aging, frequent washing), the barrier becomes weak — leading to dryness, sensitivity, and increased trans-epidermal water loss.
Label: Fact

Doc: Replenishing ceramides supports that barrier and restores moisture retention.
Label: Fact

Doc: Many dermatologists consider ceramide-infused moisturizers among the most effective options to restore skin barrier integrity, calm irritation, and enhance resilience.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: The lotion is specifically described to “help repair & restore skin’s natural barrier.”
Label: Fact

Doc: Along with ceramide-3, it includes 100% pure glycerin (a humectant that attracts and binds water to the skin) and mineral-rich Thermal Spring Water — a blend that draws moisture in and locks it within the skin, while soothing irritation.
Label: Fact

Doc: The lotion provides long-lasting hydration, helps control dryness for up to 24 hours, and reduces roughness.
Label: Fact

Doc: These all are essential for barrier recovery.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: It is non-comedogenic, pH-balanced, and formulated to be gentle, making it appropriate even for sensitive or compromised skin — situations where barrier repair is most needed.
Label: Fact

Doc: For barrier repair to be effective, regular usage — e.g., applying the lotion daily after bathing or showering — is important.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: This replenishes lipids and maintains the moisture seal.
Label: Fact

Doc: Moisturizer works best when applied on slightly damp skin — this helps trap water in the skin before sealing it with ceramides and emollients.
Label: Fact

Doc: Use gentle, non-stripping cleansers to avoid further damaging the barrier; harsh or drying soaps can undo the benefits of barrier-repair lotions.
Label: Fact

Doc: While ceramides are generally well-tolerated, individuals with very reactive skin should observe how their skin responds, especially when combining with other active ingredients or treatments.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: Ceramide-based moisturizers are not just marketing — they address fundamental skin biology.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: By replenishing essential lipids and reinforcing the skin’s protective barrier, they help reduce dryness, irritation, and sensitivity, and support long-term skin health.
Label: Fact

Doc: Hence, a well-formulated product like Ceramide-3 Deep Nourishing Body Lotion from DERMACY can indeed be an effective, clinically-sound choice for skin barrier repair — especially for dry, sensitive, or winter-affected skin.
Label: Fact

Doc: A 57-year-old man from Kolkata was travelling on the Howrah-Puri Vande Bharat Express on 4th June.
Label: Fact

Doc: The train was already 2 hours delayed (yeah, premium trains are delayed too), and when the train left Cuttack station, the passenger, Himadri Bhowmik, started to feel uneasy and fell unconscious.
Label: Fact

Doc: The railway staff were informed, but they arrived very late.
Label: Fact

Doc: They were requested to arrange for an oxygen cylinder, but there were none on the train.
Label: Fact

Doc: Some people conducted CPR on Mr Bhowmik while the train continued on its way to Bhubaneswar, without stopping at any intermediate station, so that he could be taken to the hospital.
Label: Fact

Doc: The train reached Bhubaneswar 40 minutes after he fell unconscious - there was no medical service or oxygen cylinder provided to him during this time.
Label: Fact

Doc: The train arrived at Bhubaneswar station at 1.40 PM.
Label: Fact

Doc: The train remained on the platform for 40 minutes with no TTE or Railway staff to be seen nearby.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: Other passengers desperately searched for help in the railway station, but there were no stretchers, no medical staff and no emergency protocol.
Label: Fact

Doc: Multiple videos from passengers show people pleading for help, but nobody from the Railways came forward.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: Finally, after an hour, the RPF and railway officials arrived, and he was taken to a hospital in Bhubaneswar at 2.55 PM. Unfortunately, the patient was declared dead on arrival.
Label: Fact

Doc: So yeah, here we have the most premium train in the country, which has no medical facility on board, no railway staff to handle any medical emergency.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: Mr Bhowmik could have been saved if oxygen had been supplied to him on time and if he had been rushed to a hospital by making the train stop at an intermediate station.
Label: Non-Fact

Doc: Unfortunately, he was taken to a hospital after 2 hours since he had fallen unconscious!
Label: Fact

Doc: It's very unfortunate that we aim to become a superpower, but even the best trains of our country lack basic medical facilities and staff to handle such emergencies.
Label: Non-Fact
